skill.name: managing-contacts
skill.description: Use Mailtrap Contacts API and UI guidance to create, update, import, list, segment, and enrich marketing contacts.
skill.summary: Manage Mailtrap marketing contacts, imports, lists, fields, events, and campaign audience workflows.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged scripts, shell execution, filesystem traversal, network URLs, and weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, no prompt injection, and no malicious intent; the confirmed residual risk is that the documentation shows live Mailtrap API curl requests using bearer tokens and contact data.

medium_findings.title: Live Contact API Requests With Bearer Tokens
medium_findings.description: The skill provides curl examples that POST to Mailtrap contact endpoints using MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN and contact data. This is legitimate for the skill, but users can create imports, create contacts, or trigger events if they run the commands against a real account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly visible curl examples against first-party Mailtrap endpoints. They are not auto-executed, but they can mutate account data when copied and run with valid credentials.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Findings Are Endpoint Text
low_findings.description: Static script detections at the import endpoint and section heading are false positives. The referenced lines describe Mailtrap contact imports and do not contain JavaScript import expressions or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain Markdown table text and a heading about bulk imports. There is no JavaScript dynamic import call or script execution path.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Static external command detections are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced curl examples. The file is documentation and does not execute shell, Ruby, or child process commands by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown delimiters around related skill names, placeholders, URLs, and example curl commands. No runtime file or script invokes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Point To Mailtrap Documentation And API
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are expected references to Mailtrap documentation, GitHub OpenAPI documentation, and Mailtrap API endpoints. No unknown endpoint or data exfiltration target was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed URLs are first-party Mailtrap resources or Mailtrap-maintained documentation references. The network behavior is disclosed and matches the contact-management purpose.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Ellipsis Placeholders
low_findings.description: Static filesystem findings are false positives from API path placeholders that use ellipses. The lines do not reference local files, relative filesystem paths, or traversal-sensitive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected text uses ellipses to abbreviate API endpoint paths in prose. There is no file read, file write, or filesystem path handling code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography blocker finding is a false positive on the frontmatter description line. No hash function, cipher, password handling, or cryptographic API use appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter for the skill description. The file contains documentation only and no cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manual Curl Requests Can Modify Contacts
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented POST examples use bearer authorization and real Mailtrap account paths. Users should review contact payloads and account identifiers before running the examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are legitimate examples and require user action. The risk is operational because they can modify contacts or trigger automations with valid credentials.

content.value_statement: Marketing contact lists require accurate imports, fields, events, and consent-aware segmentation. This skill guides Mailtrap Contacts API and UI workflows for clean campaign audiences.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Mailtrap Contacts API and UI workflows.
Identifies endpoints for contacts, imports, lists, fields, events, and exports.
Provides curl examples for single contact creation, bulk import, and custom events.
Clarifies contact lists, segments, custom fields, and custom events.
Distinguishes marketing contacts from sending suppressions.
Highlights rate limits and bulk import size guidance.
limitations: Does not authenticate users or retrieve Mailtrap tokens by itself.
Does not validate payloads against the OpenAPI spec automatically.
Does not execute API requests unless a user copies and runs examples.
Does not replace consent, privacy, or suppression policy review.

use_cases.title: Sync CRM Contacts
use_cases.description: Plan API-based contact creation and updates from a CRM or warehouse into Mailtrap marketing contacts.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Audiences
use_cases.description: Use lists, segments, and custom fields to organize contacts before campaign creation and scheduling.

use_cases.title: Automate Contact Events
use_cases.description: Send custom contact events with structured payloads for Mailtrap automation workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Contact Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a Mailtrap contact task
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to manage contacts in Mailtrap. Help me choose between single contact updates, bulk import, lists, fields, events, and exports.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Contact Import Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Importing a CSV or CRM audience
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mailtrap bulk contact import plan for my campaign audience. Include required inputs, payload shape, rate limits, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Custom Fields and Segments
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me map these CRM attributes to Mailtrap custom fields and segment ideas. Separate marketing eligibility from sending suppressions.

prompt_templates.title: Review an API Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Mailtrap Contacts API integration plan for token handling, account_id usage, import batching, retry behavior, and contact event payloads.

output_examples.input: I need to import 80,000 contacts from a CRM into Mailtrap.
output: Split the import into multiple jobs because the documented bulk import limit is 50,000 contacts per request.
Use the contacts import endpoint and poll import status before starting dependent campaign work.
Validate consent fields, list membership, and custom field names before sending the first request.

output_examples.input: I want to personalize campaigns by company and membership level.
output: Create custom fields for company and membership level before importing or updating contacts.
Use segments for dynamic audience selection instead of exporting and reimporting lists manually.
Keep suppression handling separate from marketing contact filters.

output_examples.input: I need to trigger an automation when a user logs in.
output: Send a contact custom event with a clear event name and a structured parameters object.
Use the contact identifier path and confirm the event schema with current Mailtrap documentation.
Test with a limited audience before enabling the automation for production contacts.

best_practices: Check the current Mailtrap OpenAPI spec before generating request bodies.
Use bulk imports for large contact loads and respect documented rate limits.
Separate marketing eligibility, list membership, and sending suppressions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production API tokens into shared prompts or logs.
Do not send one request per contact for large imports.
Do not treat marketing segments as a replacement for suppression controls.

faq.question: Does this skill run Mailtrap API calls automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. A user must run any API request with their own credentials.

faq.question: Which Mailtrap features does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers contacts, bulk imports, contact lists, custom fields, custom events, exports, and campaign audience concepts.

faq.question: Can it help with CRM synchronization?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains API workflows that can support real-time or scheduled CRM and warehouse syncs.

faq.question: Does it handle Mailtrap authentication?
faq.answer: It references token and account_id requirements, but delegates detailed authorization guidance to the related authorization skill.

faq.question: Can it validate exact request fields?
faq.answer: It instructs users to check the current Contacts OpenAPI spec before generating request bodies.

faq.question: Does it manage sending suppressions?
faq.answer: No. It explains that sending suppressions are separate from marketing contact lists and segments.
